preamble: The shift from [g] to [u] occurs through the vocalization of the voiced velar fricative [ɣ]. In Middle English, post-vocalic [g] after back vowels softened to [w] and eventually vocalized into the high back rounded vowel [u].

examples.note: The velar fricative vocalized and merged with the vowel.
Development of the modern diphthong from a vocalized velar.
